title: Respawn lock stale-reclaim is non-atomic; lock can be double-held under staleness

findingDetails.evidence: acquireRespawnLockFileAt reclaims a stale/empty lock via unlinkSync(currentLockPath) (line 668) then reopen 'wx' (673), with no atomic claim. Two launchers racing a stale lock can interleave: A unlink→A reopen(holds)→B unlink(removes A's fresh lock)→B reopen(holds), so both hold the 'exclusive' lock. The bootstrap lock avoids this with an atomic renameSync-to-staleClaim before delete (launcher lines 1272-1279); this path does not.
findingDetails.why: The launcher comment at mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:1616-1617 ('Take the exclusive respawn lock to serialize it') and respawnAfterDeadSocket both rely on this lock to serialize reap+respawn. It is not mutually exclusive when the prior holder died (stale pid), so the documented serialization contract has a hole. For spec-memory the DB double-writer is currently masked by launchServer's ownsOwnerLeaseFile gate, but the hf-model-server respawn path (acquireModelServerRespawnLockFile) relies on this lock alone and could double-spawn.
findingDetails.recommendation: Mirror the bootstrap lock: atomically rename the stale lock to a unique staleClaim path (renameSync) and only proceed if the rename succeeds, then delete the claimed copy and open 'wx'. A losing racer's rename throws ENOENT and retries, so only one launcher ever reclaims.

title: Owner-lease reclaim reread over-claims single ownership; race can leave no daemon spawned and exits silently

findingDetails.evidence: acquireOwnerLeaseFile stale path does writeOwnerLeaseFile(lease) then reread (479-489); the comment at line 478 claims this prevents two launchers both acting as owner. It only catches an interleaved writer: sequential A(write,reread=A→acquired) then B(write,reread=B→acquired) both return acquired:true with the file ending at pid B. The owner lease and respawn lock are independent/unordered, so the respawn-lock winner (A, finished acquire first) is then blocked by launchServer's ownsOwnerLeaseFile gate at line 1300 (file holds B), returns false, and main exits via 'if (!launched) return;' (line 1687) with NO JSON-RPC error emitted — unlike the writeLeaseHeldJsonRpcError bail paths.
findingDetails.why: DB single-writer is still preserved (only the owner-file pid launches), so this is not a double-writer; but it is an availability/liveness defect: in a concurrent cold-start after an owner crash with a dead daemon, the round can spawn no daemon and the respawn-lock-winner's host gets an empty stdout (broken session) rather than a retryable error. The line-478 invariant comment is misleading about what guarantees ownership.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either make the stale owner-lease reclaim atomic (claim-by-rename like the bootstrap lock, or take the respawn lock BEFORE the owner-lease reclaim so the two winners cannot diverge), and have the ownerLeaseRequired-skip branch in launchServer/main emit writeLeaseHeldJsonRpcError(...,'owner-lease-lost') before returning so the host receives a retryable response instead of a silent exit.
